item.title: Folk rock
item.description: Period1965–early 1970s; continuing currents sinceLocationLos Angeles, New York (Greenwich Village), LondonInfluencesAmerican folk revivalRock & rollBritish Invasion popBob DylanLinksRYM A twelve-string Rickenbacker picks out a chiming arpeggio over a steady backbeat, and a vocal enters in two-p...

item.title: Authenticity and its discontents
item.description: Simon Frith’s Performing Rites makes a claim that sounds simple and proves difficult to absorb: when a listener calls a record “authentic,” they are not detecting a quality the music possesses but applying a set of learned conventions about what authentic music should sound like.

item.title: Dylan at Newport
item.description: **Date: July 25, 1965 Location: Freebody Park, Newport, Rhode Island (Newport Folk Festival) Key figures: Bob Dylan, Mike Bloomfield, Al Kooper, Pete Seeger, Alan Lomax, Peter Yarrow, Albert Grossman On a Sunday evening in Newport, Bob Dylan walked onstage with a Fender Stratocaster and a five-piece...
